description: Executable evals for the sdr skill. Verifies the four-type routing surface and that the skill points at the canonical template path rather than inlining template content.
_contract_note: The skill points at ~/repos/system-design-records/ as the canonical template source. That path is an external dependency NOT owned by this repo — drift in the upstream repo (renamed templates, restructured layout) will surface as eval drift here. Re-pin the regex if the upstream template filenames change. Per ADR #0005, structural assertions (skill_invoked + tool_input_matches) are the load-bearing signal; text regex is diagnostic-tier in spirit.

evals.name: fires-on-create-sdr-trigger
evals.summary: skill fires on /sdr-style request and surfaces the four-type routing
evals.prompt: /sdr — I want to write a system design record for a new payments platform.

assertions.description: skill fires on the /sdr trigger phrase
Skill tool surface contract — skill name passed as 'sdr'
skill output references at least one of the four canonical SDR types
skill engages with SDR concepts rather than answering generically

evals.name: routes-to-system-overview-for-platform-design
evals.summary: platform-scoped prompt routes toward System Overview, not service-creation
evals.prompt: /sdr I'm designing a whole new order fulfillment platform across multiple services. Which template should I use?

assertions.description: skill fires
Skill surface contract
recommends the System Overview template for whole-system design

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-single-architectural-choice
evals.summary: /adr-shaped prompt should NOT trigger sdr — anti-trigger guardrail (paired with positive /adr signal to avoid silent-fire on empty tool stream)
evals.prompt: I need to record a single architectural decision: we're choosing Postgres over MySQL for the user service. What should I do?

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /adr (rules out empty-stream silent-fire)
sdr does NOT fire on a single-decision /adr-shaped request (diagnostic — silent-fires on zero tool uses; paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: routes-to-service-component-for-new-service
evals.summary: new-service prompt routes to Service/Component Creation template
evals.prompt: I'm building a new pricing service

assertions.description: skill fires on new-service intent
Skill surface contract
recommends Service/Component Creation template (synonym-tolerant: matches 'Service/Component Creation' prose AND 'service-component' file ref)

evals.name: routes-to-data-design-for-schema
evals.summary: schema-design prompt routes to Data Design template
evals.prompt: I need to design the order schema

assertions.description: skill fires on schema-design intent
Skill surface contract
recommends Data Design template (synonym-tolerant: matches 'Data Design', file ref 'data-design', and schema-doc paraphrases)

evals.name: routes-to-blueprint-for-reusable-pattern
evals.summary: reusable-pattern prompt routes to Blueprint template
evals.prompt: Reusable golden path for new microservices

assertions.description: skill fires on reusable-pattern intent
Skill surface contract

recommends Blueprint template (tight pattern intentional: 'Blueprint' is the template name; synonym alternation like 'reusable pattern|golden path' would risk matching prompt echo without proof of template recommendation)

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tool-adoption
evals.summary: tool-adoption prompt routes to /tech-radar, not sdr — anti-trigger guardrail paired with positive anchor
evals.prompt: Should we adopt Temporal for workflow orchestration?

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /tech-radar or names the technology radar (rules out empty-stream silent-fire); tight pattern symmetric with /adr anti-trigger
sdr does NOT fire on tool-adoption framing (diagnostic — paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tenet-deviation
evals.summary: tenet-deviation prompt routes to /tenet-exception, not sdr — anti-trigger guardrail paired with positive anchor
evals.prompt: We need to deviate from the 'all services emit OpenTelemetry' tenet

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /tenet-exception (rules out empty-stream silent-fire); tight pattern symmetric with /adr and /tech-radar anti-triggers
sdr does NOT fire on tenet-deviation framing (diagnostic — paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: fires-on-plain-english-trigger
evals.summary: plain-English (non-slash-command) trigger fires the skill — confirms description-driven surface
evals.prompt: I want to create a system design record for our new payments platform

assertions.description: skill fires on plain-English trigger phrase, no slash command
Skill surface contract

evals.name: interactive-mode-asks-which-type
evals.summary: /sdr with no args triggers interactive disambiguation across the four types

assertions.description: skill fires on bare /sdr
skill asks a disambiguation question rather than scaffolding silently
